name: modern-glass-villa
prompt: Frontal, perfectly centered, symmetrical view of a sleek two-story modern villa with an all-glass front facade set in a white concrete frame and flat roof. Include the ENTIRE BUILDING in frame from foundation/ground line to roofline — nothing cropped — with generous breathing room: blue sky with a few elegant clouds above the roof and ground/landscaping (manicured lawn and edge of the infinity pool) visible below. The villa should occupy roughly 50–70% of the frame height. Foreground: the infinity pool in front reflecting the glass facade, a tidy lawn and low paving leading toward the centered entrance; palm trees subtly flanking the composition. Middle: the full villa, perfectly aligned on the center axis, entrance/front door roughly centered. Background: Mediterranean coast visible above the roofline with the sea and distant coastline/mountains under a rich blue sky. Beautiful daytime light — warm golden-hour or late-morning sunlight casting directional, soft shadows and emphasizing white concrete texture, glass reflections, wood/metal details and pool mirror. Lush, well-maintained landscaping, no people. Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP or Hasselblad X2D 100C. Lens: tilt-shift (Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II or similar) for corrected verticals. Settings: f/8–f/11, ISO 100, tripod. For houses/villas: step back to position the camera so the complete structure plus sky and foreground are visible with space around it.

name: malibu-beach-mansion
prompt: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP, Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II tilt‑shift (or equivalent tilt‑shift 24–35mm) on tripod; f/8–f/11, ISO 100, shutter speed as required for correct exposure. Camera placed dead center on the building’s axis, perfectly frontal and level, looking straight at the facade with corrected verticals. Step well back so the entire single‑story Malibu beach mansion is fully visible from foundation/ground and sandy beach foreground to the full roofline/overhang — nothing cropped left-to-right or top-to-bottom. Building should occupy roughly 50–70% of the frame height, with generous breathing room: clear blue sky with a few elegant clouds above the roof and visible sandy beach/landscape below the base. Compose foreground with the sandy beach and low coastal planting or a simple path leading toward the centered entrance; middle ground dominated by the full horizontal massing of the house — weathered wood cladding, expansive glass, wide covered patio, and the entrance front-and-center; background reveals the Pacific Ocean directly behind the house and the horizon line above the roofline. Late‑morning or golden‑hour warm directional sunlight illuminating the facade, bringing out the wood grain, glass reflections of sky, and texture in stone/metal details. No people. Capture rich color, crisp detail, and a calm, sunlit seaside atmosphere; maintain perfect symmetry and full-structure visibility.

name: luxury-glass-skyscraper
prompt:
Photograph a 50‑story luxury residential glass skyscraper with a sleek curtain wall and planted balconies — entire tower visible from foundation to rooftop, nothing cropped. Frontal, perfectly centered, symmetrical composition: camera on the building’s center axis, looking straight at the façade. Step far back so the full building occupies roughly 50–70% of the frame height with generous breathing room: visible sky above the roofline and ground/landscaping/plaza below the base. Foreground: a compositional lead (plaza, paved drop‑off, tree‑lined promenade or reflecting pool) that leads the eye toward the centered entrance. Background: clear blue sky with a few wispy clouds and a distant skyline or landscape visible above the tower.

Lighting: beautiful daytime, golden‑warm late‑morning/golden‑hour sunlight striking the façade at an oblique angle for rich reflections and visible glass texture; balconies with plants catch warm light; no people in the scene.

Camera specs: Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP or Hasselblad X2D 100C. Lens: tilt‑shift for corrected verticals — Canon TS‑E 24mm f/3.5L II (or equivalent 24–35mm tilt/shift). For skyscrapers, step far back and use 24–35mm to fit the full tower. Settings: f/8–f/11, ISO 100, tripod, cable release or remote, shoot RAW. Ensure corrected verticals so the façade lines are parallel and distortion‑free.

Compose so the entrance/front door axis is roughly centered; capture balcony details and reflections in glass without cropping edges. Maintain lush, well‑kept landscaping, crisp material textures (glass reflections, metal mullions), and a rich blue sky with elegant clouds. Absolute constraints: full building visible roof‑to‑ground and left edge to right edge, frontal symmetrical view, generous sky and ground margin, beautiful daylight, and zero people.

name: classic-white-mansion
prompt: Frontal, perfectly centered shot of a grand neoclassical white mansion with full symmetrical wings, tall columns, circular driveway with central fountain, manicured hedges and oak trees framing the property. Absolute requirement: entire building fully visible — no cropping of roof, facade or foundation; full width visible from left edge to right edge. Camera directly on the center axis, looking straight at the façade with entrance/front door roughly centered. Generous framing: leave visible sky above the roofline and ground/landscaping below the foundation so the mansion occupies approximately 50–70% of the frame height. Foreground: circular driveway and fountain leading toward the entrance, lush lawn and hedges. Background: rich blue sky with a few elegant clouds, warm golden directional sunlight illuminating the facade to reveal stone and painted wood textures and glass reflections. No people. Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP or Hasselblad X2D 100C. Lens: tilt-shift (Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II or equivalent) for corrected verticals. Settings: f/8–f/11, ISO 100, tripod, mirror lock/remote release. Position: step back far enough to capture the whole mansion with breathing room above and below; keep camera level, no perspective tilt. Capture crisp detail, rich warm color, deep blue sky, and impeccably maintained landscaping; emphasize symmetry, classical proportions and material texture in bright daytime golden-hour light.

name: desert-modern-house
prompt:
Frontal, perfectly centered, symmetrical view of a low‑profile desert‑modern house in Scottsdale — full building visible from foundation/ground to flat rooftop/overhang, left edge to right edge, no cropping. Step far back so the house occupies roughly 50–70% of the frame height with generous breathing room: clear blue sky with a few elegant clouds above the roofline and desert ground/landscaping visible below. Foreground: a simple desert gravel path and low native landscaping with prominent saguaro cacti leading the eye toward a centered entrance. Middle: the full facade composed of warm rammed‑earth walls, weathered corten steel accents, deep flat roof overhang, and expansive floor‑to‑ceiling glass — entrance/front door roughly centered. Background: distant desert mountains and open sky visible above the roof. Lighting: warm, directional golden‑hour/late‑morning sunlight illuminating textures (stone grain, corten patina, wood), rich blue sky, crisp shadows, glass reflecting sky while remaining readable. No people. Lush, well‑maintained desert planting, clean hardscape, no clutter.

Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP or Hasselblad X2D 100C. Lens: tilt‑shift (Canon TS‑E 24mm f/3.5L II or similar) for corrected verticals. Settings: f/8–f/11, ISO 100, tripod. Camera on the center axis of the building, perfectly level, corrected verticals, generous framing, symmetrical composition. NO PEOPLE.

name: swiss-mountain-chalet
prompt: ABSOLUTE: the entire building must be visible from foundation/ground all the way to the rooftop/peak and from left edge to right edge — no cropping of any part of the structure. Frontal, perfectly centered, symmetrical composition with the camera on the building’s center axis. Generous framing: the chalet should occupy roughly 50–70% of the frame height with clear breathing room above the roof showing sky and below the base showing the alpine meadow foreground. Beautiful daytime light: warm golden-hour or late-morning sunlight from a slightly oblique angle that illuminates the dark timber upper floors and textured stone base; rich blue sky with a few elegant clouds and snow‑capped peaks visible above the roofline. Foreground: lush alpine meadow leading toward the centered entrance, subtle path or lawn and well-maintained landscaping. Middle: full traditional Swiss chalet, dark timber upper floors, stone base, large balcony with abundant flower boxes, peaked roof — entrance/front door roughly centered. Background: distant snow-capped Alps and clear sky. No people. Emphasize material textures — timber grain, stone detail, glass reflections of sky, and vibrant flowers. Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP or Hasselblad X2D 100C. Lens: tilt-shift (Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II or similar) for corrected verticals. Settings: f/8–f/11, ISO 100, tripod. For houses/villas: step back/position at a distance that fits the entire building plus space above and below so nothing is cropped. Capture crisp, high-resolution architectural detail, perfectly level and symmetrical.

name: miami-condo-tower
prompt: Frontal, perfectly centered, symmetrical composition of a curved 40-story luxury condo tower in Miami with a white, sculptural facade and flowing cantilevered balconies — the entire building must be visible from foundation/ground to rooftop/peak, left edge to right edge, with no cropping. Position the camera on the building’s central axis, directly in front, stepped far back to fit the full tower in frame; the tower should occupy roughly 50–70% of the frame height, leaving generous breathing room: clear blue sky with a few elegant clouds above the roofline and palm‑lined boulevard, landscaping and pavement visible below the base. Foreground: a palm-lined boulevard and manicured planting leading toward the entrance; entrance/front door roughly centered. Middle: full building symmetrically framed. Background: warm blue Miami sky, distant ocean/skyline hinted if visible. Lighting: beautiful daytime — late morning or golden-hour warm directional sunlight illuminating the white facade and balconies, highlighting stone texture, painted surfaces and glass reflections; no people, no vehicles. Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP or Hasselblad X2D 100C. Lens: tilt-shift (Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II or similar) for corrected verticals; for skyscrapers step far back and use 24–35mm to fit the full tower. Settings: f/8–f/11, ISO 100, tripod, mirror lockup/live view as needed. Emphasize crisp architectural detail, rich shadows, warm highlights, lush landscaping, and elegant, unobstructed framing.

name: tuscan-villa-estate
prompt: Frontal, perfectly centered, symmetrical view of a Tuscan villa — the entire building visible from foundation to rooftop and from left edge to right edge (nothing cropped). The villa sits square in the frame (camera on the center axis), occupying roughly 50–70% of the frame height with generous breathing room: clear blue sky and a few elegant clouds above the terracotta roofline and visible ground/landscaping below the base. Honey-colored stone walls, shuttered green windows, classic terracotta roof tiles, and a central arched wooden front door. Two tall cypress trees precisely flanking the entrance, a gravel path leading straight to the arched door, manicured lawn and clipped hedges in the foreground, and rolling vineyard hills visible beyond the house above the roofline as background. Warm, golden-hour late-morning/golden-hour directional sunlight illuminating the facade to reveal stone grain, wood warmth, and glass reflections — rich blue sky, no haze. No people. Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP (or Hasselblad X2D 100C). Lens: tilt-shift (Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II or similar) for corrected verticals. Settings: f/8–f/11, ISO 100, tripod. Position: step back far enough so the full villa is in frame with space around it; for houses/villas position at a distance that shows the whole building with sky above and ground below. Symmetrical composition, entrance roughly centered, lush well-maintained landscaping, crisp high-resolution architectural detail, no cropping of any part of the structure.

name: japanese-minimalist-home
prompt: Frontal, perfectly symmetrical shot of a single-story Japanese minimalist residence with clean horizontal lines, dark timber cladding and crisp white plaster walls. The entire building must be fully visible — roof/overhang to foundation and left edge to right edge — nothing cropped. Center the camera on the building’s central axis so the entrance is roughly centered; leave generous breathing room above the roofline and below the base so the house occupies roughly 50–70% of the frame height. Foreground: a slate pathway leading straight to the entrance, a meticulously composed zen rock garden and precisely trimmed trees and low planting framing the approach. Background: clear rich blue sky with a few elegant clouds visible above the roof (optionally distant treeline or low hills), ensuring sky is visible above the peak. Beautiful daytime light: late-morning or golden-hour warm directional sunlight illuminating the façade, enhancing dark timber grain, white plaster texture, slate and stone; gentle shadows to reveal depth. No people. Lush, well-maintained minimalist landscaping only. Composition: camera directly in front, on a tripod, perfectly level; use tilt-shift to correct verticals and preserve clean geometry. Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP or Hasselblad X2D 100C. Lens: tilt-shift (Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II or similar). Settings: f/8–f/11, ISO 100, tripod. Step back far enough to include the full structure and surrounding space so nothing of the building is cropped.

name: tropical-overwater-villa
prompt:
Frontal, perfectly centered architectural photograph of a luxury Maldivian overwater villa on stilts over crystal turquoise water. Entire structure fully visible — from the peak of the thatched roof down to the bases of the stilts and the water beneath — nothing cropped. Symmetrical composition with the entrance/front door centered; the wooden deck with a plunge pool clearly visible and facing the camera. Villa should occupy roughly 50–70% of the frame height, with generous breathing room above the roof (bright blue sky with a few elegant clouds) and below the stilts (glassy turquoise water and shallow reef shadows). Foreground: calm turquoise water leading toward the deck and centered walkway/steps. Middle: full villa, thatch texture, warm hardwood decking, glass reflections and stainless fittings, lush potted palms/tropical landscaping on the deck. Background: bright blue sky, a low distant atoll/horizon visible above the roofline. Warm, directional sunlight (late morning or golden hour) lighting the facade to emphasize material texture and warm tones. No people.

Camera: Phase One XF IQ4 150MP (or Hasselblad X2D 100C). Lens: tilt-shift (Canon TS-E 24mm f/3.5L II or similar) for corrected verticals. Settings: f/8–f/11, ISO 100, tripod, center-axis, perfectly level, no tilt. For villas: position at sufficient distance (use 24–35mm equivalent framing) so the full building and surrounding water are included with space above and below. Editorial-grade, pristine luxury mood, high-resolution detail of thatch, wood grain, glass reflections; warm, serene daytime atmosphere. No people.
